On Comets XI 139
The comet named Samvarta with its crest smoky and coppery
in colour, appears in the west at sunset. It occupies a third of the
sky and looks as though situated on the points of a trident. It is
dreadful in effect, as it afflicts kings with wars and strifes for so
many years as the number of Muhlirtas it remains visible, and
creates trouble for those persons in whose natal star the comet
appears.
[Here the author gives a general principle of mundane astrology
but also one of horoscopy. For, a malefic comet, like an eclipse,
rising in a person's natal asterism augurs ill for him.
